You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.178 (commit: f03d541c).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: display order bug for notes
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

Go to "card browser" then "options" and under "toggle cards/notes" select "notes". Go to "change display order" and choose another option.

### Expected behaviour

The display order should stay as set, like when "cards" is selected in "toggle cards/notes."

### Actual behaviour

When you select "notes", it automatically sets the display order to "by created time" oldest to newest. If you choose another option it doesn't change except by pressing "by created time" again to flip the direction.  This only works until you exit the app or add a new note, and it then flips to oldest to newest again.

### Debug info

```text
AnkiDroid Version = 2.16.5 (953b9bd879269910ee962b520da9705336d8bc2d)

Android Version = 13

ProductFlavor = play

Manufacturer = samsung

Model = SM-P610

Hardware = exynos9611

Webview User Agent = Mozilla/5.0 (Linux; Android 13; SM-P610 Build/TP1A.220624.014; wv) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Version/4.0 Chrome/119.0.6045.66 Safari/537.36

ACRA UUID = cc3ec77b-8b96-40ad-99e3-3187c7799494

New schema = false

Scheduler = std2

Crash Reports Enabled = true

DatabaseV2 Enabled = true
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Hi,  I'd like to work on this issue.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/model/SortType.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}
78→

 18→// "FunctionName": many libAnki functions used to have leading _s
 19→@file:Suppress("FunctionName")
 20→
 21→package com.ichi2.libanki
 22→
 23→import androidx.annotation.CheckResult
 24→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 25→import androidx.annotation.WorkerThread
 26→import anki.card_rendering.EmptyCardsReport
 27→import anki.collection.OpChanges
 28→import anki.collection.OpChangesWithCount
 29→import anki.config.ConfigKey
 30→import anki.search.SearchNode
 31→import anki.sync.SyncAuth
 32→import anki.sync.SyncStatusResponse
 33→import com.ichi2.libanki.Utils.ids2str
 34→import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.model.toBackendNote
 35→import com.ichi2.libanki.backend.model.toProtoBuf
 36→import com.ichi2.libanki.exception.ConfirmModSchemaException
 37→import com.ichi2.libanki.exception.InvalidSearchException
 38→import com.ichi2.libanki.sched.DummyScheduler
 39→import com.ichi2.libanki.sched.Scheduler
 40→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.Time
 41→import com.ichi2.libanki.utils.TimeManager
 42→import com.ichi2.utils.*
 43→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.Backend
 44→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
 45→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.exceptions.BackendInvalidInputException
 46→import timber.log.Timber
 47→import java.io.*
 48→import java.util.*
 49→
 50→// Anki maintains a cache of used tags so it can quickly present a list of tags
 51→// for autocomplete and in the browser. For efficiency, deletions are not
 52→// tracked, so unused tags can only be removed from the list with a DB check.
 53→//
 54→// This module manages the tag cache and tags for notes.
 55→@KotlinCleanup("TextUtils -> Kotlin isNotEmpty()")
 56→@KotlinCleanup("inline function in init { } so we don't need to init `crt` etc... at the definition")
 57→@KotlinCleanup("ids.size != 0")
 58→@WorkerThread
 59→open class Collection(
 60→    /**
 61→     *  @param Path The path to the collection.anki2 database. Must be unicode and openable with [File].
 62→     */
 63→    val path: String,
 64→    private var debugLog: Boolean, // Not in libAnki.
 65→    /**
 66→     * Outside of libanki, you should not access the backend directly for collection operations.
 67→     * Operations that work on a closed collection (eg importing), or do not require a collection
 68→     * at all (eg translations) are the exception.
 69→     */
 70→    val backend: Backend
 71→) {
 72→    /** Access backend translations */
 73→    val tr = backend.tr
 74→
 75→    @get:JvmName("isDbClosed")
 76→    val dbClosed: Boolean
 77→        get() {
 78→            return dbInternal == null
 79→        }
 80→
 81→    @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE)
 82→    fun debugEnsureNoOpenPointers() {
 83→        val result = backend.getActiveSequenceNumbers()
 84→        if (result.isNotEmpty()) {
 85→            val numbers = result.toString()
 86→            throw IllegalStateException("Contained unclosed sequence numbers: $numbers")
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    // a lot of legacy code does not check for nullability
 91→    val db: DB
 92→        get() = dbInternal!!
 93→
 94→    var dbInternal: DB? = null
 95→
 96→    /**
 97→     * Getters/Setters ********************************************************** *************************************
 98→     */
 99→
100→    val media: Media
101→
102→    lateinit var decks: Decks
103→        protected set
104→
105→    val tags: Tags
106→
107→    @KotlinCleanup(
108→        "move accessor methods here, maybe reconsider return type." +
109→            "See variable: conf"
110→    )
111→    lateinit var config: Config
112→
113→    @KotlinCleanup("see if we can inline a function inside init {} and make this `val`")
114→    lateinit var sched: Scheduler
115→        protected set
116→
117→    private var mStartTime: Long
118→    private var mStartReps: Int
119→
120→    val mod: Long
121→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select mod from col")
122→
123→    val crt: Long
124→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select crt from col")
125→
126→    val scm: Long
127→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select scm from col")
128→
129→    private val lastSync: Long
130→        get() = db.queryLongScalar("select ls from col")
131→
132→    fun usn(): Int {
133→        return -1
134→    }
135→
136→    var ls: Long = 0
137→    // END: SQL table columns
138→
139→    private var mLogHnd: PrintWriter? = null
140→
141→    init {
142→        media = Media(this)
143→        tags = Tags(this)
144→        val created = reopen()
145→        log(path, VersionUtils.pkgVersionName)
146→        mStartReps = 0
147→        mStartTime = 0
148→        _loadScheduler()
149→        if (created) {
150→            sched.useNewTimezoneCode()
151→            config.set("schedVer", 2)
152→            // we need to reload the scheduler: this was previously loaded as V1
153→            _loadScheduler()
154→        }
155→    }
156→
157→    fun name(): String {
158→        // TODO:
159→        return File(path).name.replace(".anki2", "")
160→    }
161→
162→    /**
163→     * Scheduler
164→     * ***********************************************************
165→     */
166→    fun schedVer(): Int {
167→        // schedVer was not set on legacy v1 collections
168→        val ver = config.get("schedVer") ?: 1
169→        return if (listOf(1, 2).contains(ver)) {
170→            ver
171→        } else {
172→            throw RuntimeException("Unsupported scheduler version")
173→        }
174→    }
175→
176→    fun _loadScheduler() {
177→        val ver = schedVer()
178→        if (ver == 1) {
179→            sched = DummyScheduler(this)
180→        } else {
181→            if (!backend.getConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021)) {
182→                backend.setConfigBool(ConfigKey.Bool.SCHED_2021, true, undoable = false)
183→            }
184→            sched = Scheduler(this)
185→            config.set("localOffset", sched.currentTimezoneOffset())
186→        }
187→    }
188→
189→    /**
190→     * Disconnect from DB.
191→     * Python implementation has a save argument for legacy reasons;
192→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.
193→     */
194→    @Synchronized
195→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
196→        if (!dbClosed) {
197→            if (!forFullSync) {
198→                backend.closeCollection(downgrade)
199→            }
200→            dbInternal = null
201→            _closeLog()
202→            Timber.i("Collection closed")
203→        }
204→    }
205→
206→    /** True if DB was created */
207→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
208→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
209→        return if (dbClosed) {
210→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
211→            dbInternal = db_
212→            load()
213→            _openLog()
214→            if (afterFullSync) {
215→                _loadScheduler()
216→            }
217→            created
218→        } else {
219→            false
220→        }
221→    }
222→
223→    fun load() {
224→        notetypes = Notetypes(this)
225→        decks = Decks(this)
226→        config = Config(backend)
227→    }
228→
229→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
230→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
231→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
232→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
233→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
234→     */
235→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
236→        db.execute(
237→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
238→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
239→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
240→        )
241→    }
242→
243→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
244→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
245→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
246→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
247→     *
248→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
249→     */
250→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
251→    fun modSchema() {
252→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
253→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
254→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
255→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
256→             code to handle the user's choice */
257→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
258→        }
259→        modSchemaNoCheck()
260→    }
261→
262→    /** True if schema changed since last sync.  */
263→    fun schemaChanged(): Boolean {
264→        return scm > lastSync
265→    }
266→
267→    /**
268→     * Object creation helpers **************************************************
269→     * *********************************************
270→     */
271→    fun getCard(id: Long): Card {
272→        return Card(this, id)
273→    }
274→
275→    fun updateCards(cards: Iterable<Card>, skipUndoEntry: Boolean = false): OpChanges {
276→        return backend.updateCards(cards.map { it.toBackendCard() }, skipUndoEntry)
277→    }
278→
279→    fun updateCard(card: Card, skipUndoEntry: Boolean = false): OpChanges {
280→        return updateCards(listOf(card), skipUndoEntry)
281→    }
282→
283→    fun getNote(id: Long): Note {
284→        return Note(this, id)
285→    }
286→
287→    /**
288→     * Notes ******************************************************************** ***************************
289→     */
290→    fun noteCount(): Int {
291→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM notes")
292→    }
293→
294→    /**
295→     * Return a new note with the model derived from the deck or the configuration
296→     * @param forDeck When true it uses the model specified in the deck (mid), otherwise it uses the model specified in
297→     * the configuration (curModel)
298→     * @return The new note
299→     */
300→    fun newNote(forDeck: Boolean = true): Note {
301→        return newNote(notetypes.current(forDeck))
302→    }
303→
304→    /**
305→     * Return a new note with a specific model
306→     * @param m The model to use for the new note
307→     * @return The new note
308→     */
309→    fun newNote(m: NotetypeJson): Note {
310→        return Note(this, m)
311→    }
312→
313→    /**
314→     * Cards ******************************************************************** ***************************
315→     */
316→    val isEmpty: Boolean
317→        get() = db.queryScalar("SELECT 1 FROM cards LIMIT 1") == 0
318→
319→    fun cardCount(): Int {
320→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM cards")
321→    }
322→
323→    // NOT IN LIBANKI //
324→    fun cardCount(vararg dids: Long): Int {
325→        return db.queryScalar("SELECT count() FROM cards WHERE did IN " + Utils.ids2str(dids))
326→    }
327→
328→    fun isEmptyDeck(vararg dids: Long): Boolean {
329→        return cardCount(*dids) == 0
330→    }
331→
332→    /*
333→      Finding cards ************************************************************ ***********************************
334→     */
335→
336→    /**
337→     * Construct a search string from the provided search nodes. For example:
338→     * */
339→    /*
340→            import anki.search.searchNode
341→            import anki.search.SearchNode
342→            import anki.search.SearchNodeKt.group
343→
344→            val node = searchNode {
345→                group = SearchNodeKt.group {
346→                    joiner = SearchNode.Group.Joiner.AND
347→                    nodes += searchNode { deck = "a **test** deck" }
348→                    nodes += searchNode {
349→                        negated = searchNode {
350→                            tag = "foo"
351→                        }
352→                    }
353→                    nodes += searchNode { flag = SearchNode.Flag.FLAG_GREEN }
354→                }
355→            }
356→            // yields "deck:a \*\*test\*\* deck" -tag:foo flag:3
357→            val text = col.buildSearchString(node)
358→        }
359→    */
360→    fun buildSearchString(node: SearchNode): String {
361→        return backend.buildSearchString(node)
362→    }
363→
364→    fun findCards(
365→        search: String,
366→        order: SortOrder
367→    ): List<Long> {
368→        val adjustedOrder = if (order is SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering) {
369→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
370→                config.get("sortType") ?: "noteFld",
371→                config.get("sortBackwards") ?: false
372→            )
373→        } else {
374→            order
375→        }
376→        val cardIdsList = try {
377→            backend.searchCards(search, adjustedOrder.toProtoBuf())
378→        } catch (e: BackendInvalidInputException) {
379→            throw InvalidSearchException(e)
380→        }
381→        return cardIdsList
382→    }
383→
384→    fun findNotes(
385→        query: String,
386→        order: SortOrder = SortOrder.NoOrdering()
387→    ): List<Long> {
388→        val adjustedOrder = if (order is SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering) {
389→            SortOrder.BuiltinSortKind(
390→                config.get("noteSortType") ?: "noteFld",
391→                config.get("browserNoteSortBackwards") ?: false
392→            )
393→        } else {
394→            order
395→        }
396→        val noteIDsList = try {
397→            backend.searchNotes(query, adjustedOrder.toProtoBuf())
398→        } catch (e: BackendInvalidInputException) {
399→            throw InvalidSearchException(e)
400→        }
401→        return noteIDsList
402→    }
403→
404→    /** Return a list of card ids  */
405→    @KotlinCleanup("set reasonable defaults")
406→    fun findCards(search: String): List<Long> {
407→        return findCards(search, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
408→    }
409→
410→    /** Return a list of card ids  */
411→    @RustCleanup("Remove in V16.") // Not in libAnki
412→    fun findOneCardByNote(query: String): List<CardId> {
413→        // This function shouldn't exist and CardBrowser should be modified to use Notes,
414→        // so not much effort was expended here
415→
416→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
417→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
418→        return db.queryLongList(
419→            """
420→SELECT c.id
421→FROM (
422→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
423→  FROM cards
424→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
425→  GROUP BY nid
426→) AS card_with_min_ord
427→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
428→            """.trimMargin()
429→        )
430→    }

432→    @RustCleanup("Calling code should handle returned OpChanges")
433→    fun findReplace(nids: List<Long>, src: String, dst: String, regex: Boolean = false, field: String? = null, fold: Boolean = true): Int {
434→        return backend.findAndReplace(nids, src, dst, regex, !fold, field ?: "").count
435→    }

444→    /*
445→     * Timeboxing *************************************************************** ********************************
446→     */
447→
448→    fun startTimebox() {
449→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
450→        mStartReps = sched.reps
451→    }
452→
453→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
454→
455→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
456→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
457→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
458→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
459→            // timeboxing disabled
460→            return null
461→        }
462→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
463→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
464→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
465→            TimeboxReached(
466→                limit,
467→                sched.reps - mStartReps
468→            ).also {
469→                startTimebox()
470→            }
471→        } else {
472→            null
473→        }
474→    }
475→

477→     * Undo ********************************************************************* **************************
478→     */
479→
480→    /** eg "Undo suspend card" if undo available */
481→    fun undoLabel(): String? {
482→        val action = undoStatus().undo
483→        return action?.let { tr.undoUndoAction(it) }
484→    }
485→
486→    fun undoAvailable(): Boolean {
487→        val status = undoStatus()
488→        return status.undo != null
489→    }
490→

492→        val action = undoStatus().redo
493→        return action?.let { tr.undoRedoAction(it) }
494→    }
495→

497→        return undoStatus().redo != null
498→    }

501→    fun remNotes(ids: LongArray) {
502→        removeNotes(nids = ids.asIterable())
503→    }

506→        return backend.removeNotes(noteIds = nids, cardIds = cids)
507→    }

514→        val resp = backend.addNote(note.toBackendNote(), deckId)
515→        note.id = resp.noteId
516→        return resp.changes
517→    }

519→    /** allowEmpty is ignored in the new schema */
520→    @RustCleanup("Remove this in favour of addNote() above; call addNote() inside undoableOp()")
521→    fun addNote(note: Note): Int {
522→        addNote(note, note.notetype.did)
523→        return note.numberOfCards()
524→    }

526→    /**
527→     * DB maintenance *********************************************************** ************************************
528→     */
529→    /*
530→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
531→     */
532→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
533→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
534→        // cards without notes
535→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
536→            return false
537→        }
538→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
539→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
540→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
541→        ) > 0
542→        // notes without cards or models
543→        if (badNotes) {
544→            return false
545→        }
546→        // invalid ords
547→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
548→            // ignore clozes
549→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
550→                continue
551→            }
552→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
553→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
554→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
555→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
556→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
557→                tmpls.length(),
558→                m.getLong("id")
559→            ) > 0
560→            if (badOrd) {
561→                return false
562→            }
563→        }
564→        return true
565→    }
566→
567→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
568→        if (!debugLog) return
569→
570→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
571→
572→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
573→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
574→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
575→
576→        val objectsString = objects
577→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
578→            .joinToString(", ")
579→
580→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
584→        mLogHnd?.let {
585→            try {
586→                it.println(s)
587→            } catch (e: Exception) {
588→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
589→            }
590→        }
591→        Timber.d(s)
592→    }
593→
594→    private fun _openLog() {
595→        if (!debugLog) {
596→            return
597→        }
598→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
599→        try {
600→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
601→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
602→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
603→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
604→                    lpath2.delete()
605→                }
606→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
607→            }
608→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
609→        } catch (e: IOException) {
610→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
611→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
612→            debugLog = false
613→        }
614→    }
615→
616→    private fun _closeLog() {
617→        if (!debugLog) return
618→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
619→        mLogHnd?.close()
620→        mLogHnd = null
621→    }
622→

626→    fun setUserFlag(flag: Int, cids: List<Long>) {
627→        assert(flag in (0..7))
628→        db.execute(
629→            "update cards set flags = (flags & ~?) | ?, usn=?, mod=? where id in " + Utils.ids2str(
630→                cids
631→            ),
632→            7,
633→            flag,
634→            usn(),
635→            TimeManager.time.intTime()
636→        )
637→    }

697→    fun notesOfCards(cids: Iterable<CardId>): List<NoteId> {

699→    }

701→    /**
702→     * returns the list of cloze ordinals in a note
703→     *
704→     * `"{{c1::A}} {{c3::B}}" => [1, 3]`
705→     */
706→    fun clozeNumbersInNote(n: Note): List<Int> {
707→        // the call appears to be non-deterministic. Sort ascending

709→            .sorted()

content.text:
Now I understand the issue! The problem is in the `changeCardOrder` function in `CardBrowser.kt`. 

When changing the display order:
1. It saves the sort type using `which.save(getColUnsafe.config, baseContext.sharedPrefs())` which sets `sortType` in the config
2. It sets `sortBackwards` for the ascending/descending order

However, when the user is in "Notes" mode, the code should be saving to `noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards` instead of `sortType` and `sortBackwards`.

Looking at `Collection.kt`, I can see:
- For cards: `findCards` uses `config.get("sortType")` and `config.get("sortBackwards")`
- For notes: `findNotes` uses `config.get("noteSortType")` and `config.get("browserNoteSortBackwards")`

But in `CardBrowser.kt`, the `changeCardOrder` function always saves to `sortType` and `sortBackwards`, regardless of whether the user is in Cards or Notes mode. This is the bug!

Let me also check the `onCollectionLoaded` function to see how the order is loaded:

19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")

content.text:
Now I understand the issue completely. The problem is:

1. In `changeCardOrder`, the code always saves to `sortType` and `sortBackwards` config keys
2. In `onCollectionLoaded`, the code always loads from `sortType` and `sortBackwards` config keys
3. But when in Notes mode, the backend's `findNotes` function reads from `noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards`

The fix needs to:
1. Modify `changeCardOrder` to save to the correct config keys based on whether we're in Cards or Notes mode
2. Modify `onCollectionLoaded` to load from the correct config keys based on the current mode
3. Potentially modify `SortType.save()` and `SortType.fromCol()` to support both modes

Let me implement the fix:

17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}

content.text:
I see the issue now. In `onCollectionLoaded`:
1. First, `mOrder` and `mOrderAsc` are loaded from the config (lines 469-470)
2. Then, `cardsOrNotes` is set from the collection (line 584)

But the problem is that `mOrder` and `mOrderAsc` are loaded BEFORE `cardsOrNotes` is set. So when loading, we don't know yet whether we're in cards or notes mode.

The fix needs to:
1. Move the loading of `cardsOrNotes` before loading the sort order
2. Pass the `cardsOrNotes` mode to `SortType.fromCol()` and when loading `mOrderAsc`

Let me update the `onCollectionLoaded` function:

function.name: run_terminal_cmd
function.arguments:
{"command": "cat > /tmp/fix_cardbrowser.py << 'EOF'
import re

with open('/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()

# Fix 1: Update onCollectionLoaded to load cardsOrNotes first, then use it for sort order
old_pattern = '''    // Finish initializing the activity after the collection has been correctly loaded
    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: com.ichi2.libanki.Collection) {
        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
        Timber.d("onCollectionLoaded()")
        registerExternalStorageListener()
        val preferences = baseContext.sharedPrefs()

        mOrder = SortType.fromCol(col.config, preferences)
        mOrderAsc = col.config.get("sortBackwards") ?: false'''

new_pattern = '''    // Finish initializing the activity after the collection has been correctly loaded
    override fun onCollectionLoaded(col: com.ichi2.libanki.Collection) {
        super.onCollectionLoaded(col)
        Timber.d("onCollectionLoaded()")
        registerExternalStorageListener()
        val preferences = baseContext.sharedPrefs()

        cardsOrNotes = CardsOrNotes.fromCollection(col)
        mOrder = SortType.fromCol(col.config, preferences, cardsOrNotes == NOTES)
        mOrderAsc = col.config.get(if (cardsOrNotes == NOTES) "browserNoteSortBackwards" else "sortBackwards") ?: false'''

content = content.replace(old_pattern, new_pattern)

# Fix 2: Remove the duplicate cardsOrNotes assignment later in the function
old_cardsOrNotes = '''        cardsOrNotes = CardsOrNotes.fromCollection(col)
        isTruncated'''
new_cardsOrNotes = '''        isTruncated'''

content = content.replace(old_cardsOrNotes, new_cardsOrNotes)

with open('/project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt', 'w') as f:
    f.write(content)

print("Done!")
EOF

17→package com.ichi2.anki.model
18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        if (isNotesMode) {
54→            config.set("noteSortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
55→        } else {
56→            config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
57→        }
58→        preferences.edit {
59→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
60→        }
61→    }
62→
63→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
64→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
65→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
66→
67→    companion object {
68→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false): SortType {
69→            val colOrder = if (isNotesMode) {
70→                config.get<String>("noteSortType")
71→            } else {
72→                config.get<String>("sortType")
73→            }
74→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
75→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
76→                return NO_SORTING
77→            }
78→            return type
79→        }
80→
81→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
82→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
83→        }
84→    }
85→}
86→

---
name: firebender-help
description: Official Firebender documentation and configuration guide. CRITICAL - Firebender is a DIFFERENT coding agent than Cursor or Claude Code. Use this skill whenever the user asks about Firebender configuration, features, commands, or functionality. If the user is trying to create a skill, agents.md, rules, sub agent, configure MCP, and more, you should likely consider using this skill because the context will likely be relevant for what the user wants in order to execute it.
version: 0.15.4
---

# Firebender Help & Documentation

## About Firebender

Firebender is the best coding agent for Android engineers and JetBrains IDEs. It's purpose-built for Android Studio and IntelliJ IDEA with deep integrations for Jetpack Compose, Gradle, and mobile development workflows. It works great for all languages and all jetbrains IDEs too.

## CRITICAL: Firebender is NOT Cursor or Claude Code

**DO NOT make assumptions about Firebender configuration based on other tools.**

Firebender has its own unique:
- Configuration system (firebender.json)
- Command syntax
- Rules system
- MCP integration
- Feature set
- Jetbrains LSP tools (go-to-definition, find usages, renaming symbols, etc.)
- Android-specific tooling

## Mandatory Workflow for Helping Users

When helping with Firebender questions, you MUST:

### Step 1: Read the Documentation First
Use the Read tool to access the relevant doc file from this skill's `docs/` directory.

### Step 2: Cite Your Sources
Quote the EXACT lines from the documentation that answer the user's question.
Show the precise configuration syntax or command usage from the docs.

### Step 3: Implement or Answer
Only after citing documentation sources, proceed with:
- Creating configuration files
- Running commands
- Answering questions
- Making changes

## Available Documentation

The documentation is organized in the `docs/` subdirectory. Use the `docs.json` file to understand the complete navigation structure and find relevant pages.

## How to Access Documentation

The documentation files are extracted to `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/firebender-help-0.15.4/docs/` on first use.

To read a doc file:
1. Identify which doc is relevant by reading `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json`
2. Use the Read tool with the path: `<skill-base-path>/docs/<path-to-file>`

The base directory path is shown when this skill is invoked - look for "Base directory for this skill:" in the tool result.

**Note:** Built-in Firebender skills are cached in `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/`, while user-defined skills go in `~/.firebender/skills/`.

## Linking to Online Documentation

When citing documentation, provide web URLs so users can reference them online.

**URL Pattern:** `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-path>`

The `<page-path>` comes from the "pages" entries in `docs.json`. To construct URLs:
1. Read `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json` to see the navigation structure
2. Find the page entry that matches the documentation file (without .mdx/.md extension)
3. Construct the URL as `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-entry>`

## Version Lock

This documentation matches Firebender plugin version **0.15.4**.
User's plugin version may differ - configurations could vary between versions.

## Key Principle

**Sources first, implementation second.**
Never guess Firebender's behavior - always verify with bundled documentation first.

content.text:
Now let me provide a summary of the changes I made to fix this issue:

## Summary

The issue was that when the Card Browser was in "Notes" mode, the display order settings were not being persisted correctly. The root cause was that the code was always saving/loading the sort configuration to/from `sortType` and `sortBackwards` config keys, but the backend's `findNotes` function actually uses `noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards` keys for notes mode.

### Changes Made:

1. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/model/SortType.kt`**:
   - Added `isNotesMode` parameter to `save()` method - saves to `noteSortType` when in notes mode, `sortType` otherwise
   - Added `isNotesMode` parameter to `fromCol()` method - loads from `noteSortType` when in notes mode, `sortType` otherwise

2. **`AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowser.kt`**:
   - In `onCollectionLoaded()`: Moved `cardsOrNotes` loading before sort order loading, and now uses the correct config keys based on mode
   - In `changeCardOrder()`: Now saves to the correct config keys (`browserNoteSortBackwards` for notes mode, `sortBackwards` for cards mode)

3. **`AnkiDroid/src/test/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardBrowserNotesModeSortTest.kt`** (new file):
   - Added 6 tests to verify the fix works correctly for both cards and notes modes

### Test Results:
- All 6 new tests pass
- Existing CardBrowser tests continue to pass
- Build compiles successfully

content.text:
The fix is complete. Let me provide a final summary:

## Issue Fixed: Display Order Bug for Notes in Card Browser

### Problem
When the Card Browser was in "Notes" mode, changing the display order didn't persist correctly. The settings would reset to "by created time" oldest to newest when exiting the app or adding a new note.

### Root Cause
The Card Browser was always saving and loading sort order settings to/from `sortType` and `sortBackwards` config keys, regardless of whether it was in "Cards" or "Notes" mode. However, the backend's `findNotes` function uses different config keys: `noteSortType` and `browserNoteSortBackwards`.

### Changes Made

1. **`SortType.kt`**: Added `isNotesMode` parameter to both `save()` and `fromCol()` methods to use the correct config keys based on the current mode.

2. **`CardBrowser.kt`**: 
   - Modified `onCollectionLoaded()` to load `cardsOrNotes` first, then load sort settings from the appropriate config keys
   - Modified `changeCardOrder()` to save settings to the appropriate config keys based on the current mode

3. **New test file** `CardBrowserNotesModeSortTest.kt`: Added 6 comprehensive tests to verify the fix works correctly.

### Verification
- All 6 new tests pass ✓
- Existing CardBrowser tests pass ✓
- Build compiles successfully ✓
- No lint errors ✓

189→    /**
190→     * Disconnect from DB.
191→     * Python implementation has a save argument for legacy reasons;
192→     * AnkiDroid always saves as changes are made.
193→     */
194→    @Synchronized
195→    fun close(downgrade: Boolean = false, forFullSync: Boolean = false) {
196→        if (!dbClosed) {
197→            if (!forFullSync) {
198→                backend.closeCollection(downgrade)
199→            }
200→            dbInternal = null
201→            _closeLog()
202→            Timber.i("Collection closed")
203→        }
204→    }

206→    /** True if DB was created */
207→    fun reopen(afterFullSync: Boolean = false): Boolean {
208→        Timber.i("(Re)opening Database: %s", path)
209→        return if (dbClosed) {
210→            val (db_, created) = Storage.openDB(path, backend, afterFullSync)
211→            dbInternal = db_
212→            load()
213→            _openLog()
214→            if (afterFullSync) {
215→                _loadScheduler()
216→            }
217→            created
218→        } else {
219→            false
220→        }
221→    }

229→    /** Note: not in libanki.  Mark schema modified to force a full
230→     * sync, but with the confirmation checking function disabled This
231→     * is equivalent to `modSchema(False)` in Anki. A distinct method
232→     * is used so that the type does not states that an exception is
233→     * thrown when in fact it is never thrown.
234→     */
235→    open fun modSchemaNoCheck() {
236→        db.execute(
237→            "update col set scm=?, mod=?",
238→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS(),
239→            TimeManager.time.intTimeMS()
240→        )
241→    }
242→
243→    /** Mark schema modified to force a full sync.
244→     * ConfirmModSchemaException will be thrown if the user needs to be prompted to confirm the action.
245→     * If the user chooses to confirm then modSchemaNoCheck should be called, after which the exception can
246→     * be safely ignored, and the outer code called again.
247→     *
248→     * @throws ConfirmModSchemaException
249→     */
250→    @Throws(ConfirmModSchemaException::class)
251→    fun modSchema() {
252→        if (!schemaChanged()) {
253→            /* In Android we can't show a dialog which blocks the main UI thread
254→             Therefore we can't wait for the user to confirm if they want to do
255→             a full sync here, and we instead throw an exception asking the outer
256→             code to handle the user's choice */
257→            throw ConfirmModSchemaException()
258→        }
259→        modSchemaNoCheck()
260→    }

414→        // so not much effort was expended here
415→
416→        val noteIds = findNotes(query, SortOrder.NoOrdering())
417→        // select the card with the lowest `ord` to show
418→        return db.queryLongList(
419→            """
420→SELECT c.id
421→FROM (
422→  SELECT nid, MIN(ord) AS ord
423→  FROM cards
424→  WHERE nid IN ${Utils.ids2str(noteIds)} 
425→  GROUP BY nid
426→) AS card_with_min_ord
427→JOIN cards AS c ON card_with_min_ord.nid = c.nid AND card_with_min_ord.ord = c.ord;
428→            """.trimMargin()
429→        )
430→    }

447→
448→    fun startTimebox() {
449→        mStartTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
450→        mStartReps = sched.reps
451→    }
452→
453→    data class TimeboxReached(val secs: Int, val reps: Int)
454→
455→    /* Return (elapsedTime, reps) if timebox reached, or null.
456→    * Automatically restarts timebox if expired. */
457→    fun timeboxReached(): TimeboxReached? {
458→        if (sched.timeboxSecs() == 0) {
459→            // timeboxing disabled
460→            return null
461→        }
462→        val elapsed = TimeManager.time.intTime() - mStartTime
463→        val limit = sched.timeboxSecs()
464→        return if (elapsed > limit) {
465→            TimeboxReached(
466→                limit,
467→                sched.reps - mStartReps
468→            ).also {
469→                startTimebox()
470→            }
471→        } else {
472→            null
473→        }
474→    }

529→    /*
530→     * Basic integrity check. Only used by unit tests.
531→     */
532→    @KotlinCleanup("have getIds() return a list of mids and define idsToStr over it")
533→    fun basicCheck(): Boolean {
534→        // cards without notes
535→        if (db.queryScalar("select 1 from cards where nid not in (select id from notes) limit 1") > 0) {
536→            return false
537→        }
538→        val badNotes = db.queryScalar(
539→            "select 1 from notes where id not in (select distinct nid from cards) " +
540→                "or mid not in " + Utils.ids2str(notetypes.ids()) + " limit 1"
541→        ) > 0
542→        // notes without cards or models
543→        if (badNotes) {
544→            return false
545→        }
546→        // invalid ords
547→        for (m in notetypes.all()) {
548→            // ignore clozes
549→            if (m.getInt("type") != Consts.MODEL_STD) {
550→                continue
551→            }
552→            // Make a list of valid ords for this model
553→            val tmpls = m.getJSONArray("tmpls")
554→            val badOrd = db.queryScalar(
555→                "select 1 from cards where (ord < 0 or ord >= ?) and nid in ( " +
556→                    "select id from notes where mid = ?) limit 1",
557→                tmpls.length(),
558→                m.getLong("id")
559→            ) > 0
560→            if (badOrd) {
561→                return false
562→            }
563→        }
564→        return true
565→    }

567→    fun log(vararg objects: Any?) {
568→        if (!debugLog) return
569→
570→        val unixTime = TimeManager.time.intTime()
571→
572→        val outerTraceElement = Thread.currentThread().stackTrace[3]
573→        val fileName = outerTraceElement.fileName
574→        val methodName = outerTraceElement.methodName
575→
576→        val objectsString = objects
577→            .map { if (it is LongArray) Arrays.toString(it) else it }
578→            .joinToString(", ")
579→
580→        writeLog("[$unixTime] $fileName:$methodName() $objectsString")
581→    }
582→
583→    private fun writeLog(s: String) {
584→        mLogHnd?.let {
585→            try {
586→                it.println(s)
587→            } catch (e: Exception) {
588→                Timber.w(e, "Failed to write to collection log")
589→            }
590→        }
591→        Timber.d(s)
592→    }
593→
594→    private fun _openLog() {
595→        if (!debugLog) {
596→            return
597→        }
598→        Timber.i("Opening Collection Log")
599→        try {
600→            val lpath = File(path.replaceFirst("\\.anki2$".toRegex(), ".log"))
601→            if (lpath.exists() && lpath.length() > 10 * 1024 * 1024) {
602→                val lpath2 = File("$lpath.old")
603→                if (lpath2.exists()) {
604→                    lpath2.delete()
605→                }
606→                lpath.renameTo(lpath2)
607→            }
608→            mLogHnd = PrintWriter(BufferedWriter(FileWriter(lpath, true)), true)
609→        } catch (e: IOException) {
610→            // turn off logging if we can't open the log file
611→            Timber.e("Failed to open collection.log file - disabling logging")
612→            debugLog = false
613→        }
614→    }
615→
616→    private fun _closeLog() {
617→        if (!debugLog) return
618→        Timber.i("Closing Collection Log")
619→        mLogHnd?.close()
620→        mLogHnd = null
621→    }

531→        cardsListView.setOnItemClickListener { _: AdapterView<*>?, view: View?, position: Int, _: Long ->

29→    NOTES;
30→
31→    fun saveToCollection(collection: Collection) {
32→        collection.config.setBool(ConfigKey.Bool.BROWSER_TABLE_SHOW_NOTES_MODE, this == NOTES)

35→    companion object {
36→        fun fromCollection(col: Collection): CardsOrNotes =
37→            when (col.config.getBool(ConfigKey.Bool.BROWSER_TABLE_SHOW_NOTES_MODE)) {
38→                true -> NOTES
39→                false -> CARDS
40→            }
41→    }

17→package com.ichi2.anki.dialogs
18→
19→import android.app.Dialog
20→import android.content.DialogInterface
21→import android.os.Bundle
22→import android.view.View
23→import android.widget.CheckBox
24→import android.widget.RadioButton
25→import android.widget.RadioGroup
26→import androidx.annotation.IdRes
27→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatDialogFragment
28→import com.google.android.material.dialog.MaterialAlertDialogBuilder
29→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
30→import com.ichi2.anki.R
31→import com.ichi2.anki.model.CardsOrNotes
32→
33→class BrowserOptionsDialog(private val cardsOrNotes: CardsOrNotes, private val isTruncated: Boolean) : AppCompatDialogFragment() {
34→    private lateinit var dialogView: View
35→
36→    private val positiveButtonClick = { _: DialogInterface, _: Int ->
37→        @IdRes val selectedButtonId = dialogView.findViewById<RadioGroup>(R.id.select_browser_mode).checkedRadioButtonId
38→        val newCardsOrNotes = if (selectedButtonId == R.id.select_cards_mode) CardsOrNotes.CARDS else CardsOrNotes.NOTES
39→        if (cardsOrNotes != newCardsOrNotes) {
40→            (activity as CardBrowser).switchCardOrNote(newCardsOrNotes)
41→        }
42→        val newTruncate = dialogView.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.truncate_checkbox).isChecked
43→
44→        if (newTruncate != isTruncated) {
45→            (activity as CardBrowser).onTruncate(newTruncate)
46→        }
47→    }
48→
49→    override fun onCreateDialog(savedInstanceState: Bundle?): Dialog {
50→        val layoutInflater = requireActivity().layoutInflater
51→        dialogView = layoutInflater.inflate(R.layout.browser_options_dialog, null)
52→
53→        if (cardsOrNotes == CardsOrNotes.CARDS) {
54→            dialogView.findViewById<RadioButton>(R.id.select_cards_mode).isChecked = true
55→        } else {
56→            dialogView.findViewById<RadioButton>(R.id.select_notes_mode).isChecked = true
57→        }
58→
59→        dialogView.findViewById<CheckBox>(R.id.truncate_checkbox).isChecked = isTruncated
60→
61→        return MaterialAlertDialogBuilder(requireContext()).run {
62→            this.setView(dialogView)
63→            this.setTitle(getString(R.string.browser_options_dialog_heading))
64→            this.setNegativeButton(getString(R.string.dialog_cancel)) { _: DialogInterface, _: Int ->
65→                dismiss()
66→            }
67→            this.setPositiveButton(getString(R.string.dialog_ok), DialogInterface.OnClickListener(function = positiveButtonClick))
68→            this.create()
69→        }
70→    }
71→}

18→
19→import android.content.SharedPreferences
20→import androidx.core.content.edit
21→import com.ichi2.anki.CardBrowser
22→import com.ichi2.anki.R
23→import com.ichi2.libanki.Config
24→import com.ichi2.libanki.SortOrder
25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
54→        preferences.edit {
55→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
56→        }
57→    }
58→
59→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
60→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
61→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
62→
63→    companion object {
64→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences): SortType {
65→            val colOrder = config.get<String>("sortType")
66→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
67→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
68→                return NO_SORTING
69→            }
70→            return type
71→        }
72→
73→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
74→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
75→        }
76→    }
77→}

25→
26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        if (isNotesMode) {
54→            config.set("noteSortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
55→        } else {
56→            config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
57→        }
58→        preferences.edit {
59→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
60→        }
61→    }
62→
63→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
64→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
65→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
66→
67→    companion object {
68→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false): SortType {
69→            val colOrder = if (isNotesMode) {
70→                config.get<String>("noteSortType")
71→            } else {
72→                config.get<String>("sortType")
73→            }
74→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
75→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
76→                return NO_SORTING
77→            }
78→            return type
79→        }
80→
81→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
82→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
83→        }
84→    }
85→}

# Firebender Help & Documentation

## About Firebender

Firebender is the best coding agent for Android engineers and JetBrains IDEs. It's purpose-built for Android Studio and IntelliJ IDEA with deep integrations for Jetpack Compose, Gradle, and mobile development workflows. It works great for all languages and all jetbrains IDEs too.

## CRITICAL: Firebender is NOT Cursor or Claude Code

**DO NOT make assumptions about Firebender configuration based on other tools.**

Firebender has its own unique:
- Configuration system (firebender.json)
- Command syntax
- Rules system
- MCP integration
- Feature set
- Jetbrains LSP tools (go-to-definition, find usages, renaming symbols, etc.)
- Android-specific tooling

## Mandatory Workflow for Helping Users

When helping with Firebender questions, you MUST:

### Step 1: Read the Documentation First
Use the Read tool to access the relevant doc file from this skill's `docs/` directory.

### Step 2: Cite Your Sources
Quote the EXACT lines from the documentation that answer the user's question.
Show the precise configuration syntax or command usage from the docs.

### Step 3: Implement or Answer
Only after citing documentation sources, proceed with:
- Creating configuration files
- Running commands
- Answering questions
- Making changes

## Available Documentation

The documentation is organized in the `docs/` subdirectory. Use the `docs.json` file to understand the complete navigation structure and find relevant pages.

## How to Access Documentation

The documentation files are extracted to `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/firebender-help-0.15.4/docs/` on first use.

To read a doc file:
1. Identify which doc is relevant by reading `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json`
2. Use the Read tool with the path: `<skill-base-path>/docs/<path-to-file>`

The base directory path is shown when this skill is invoked - look for "Base directory for this skill:" in the tool result.

**Note:** Built-in Firebender skills are cached in `~/.firebender/.cache/skills/`, while user-defined skills go in `~/.firebender/skills/`.

## Linking to Online Documentation

When citing documentation, provide web URLs so users can reference them online.

**URL Pattern:** `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-path>`

The `<page-path>` comes from the "pages" entries in `docs.json`. To construct URLs:
1. Read `<skill-base-path>/docs/docs.json` to see the navigation structure
2. Find the page entry that matches the documentation file (without .mdx/.md extension)
3. Construct the URL as `https://docs.firebender.com/<page-entry>`

## Version Lock

This documentation matches Firebender plugin version **0.15.4**.
User's plugin version may differ - configurations could vary between versions.

## Key Principle

**Sources first, implementation second.**
Never guess Firebender's behavior - always verify with bundled documentation first.

26→/**
27→ * How to sort the rows in the [CardBrowser]
28→ *
29→ * This is an adapter from our [SharedPreferences] based handling of sorting
30→ * to Anki's [Config]
31→ *
32→ * We can likely remove the SharedPreferences and rely entirely on Anki
33→ *
34→ * @param ankiSortType The value to be passed into Anki's "sortType" config
35→ * @param cardBrowserLabelIndex The index into [R.array.card_browser_order_labels]
36→ */
37→@Suppress("unused") // 'unused' entries are iterated over by .entries
38→enum class SortType(val ankiSortType: String?, val cardBrowserLabelIndex: Int) {
39→    NO_SORTING(null, 0),
40→    SORT_FIELD("noteFld", 1),
41→    CREATED_TIME("noteCrt", 2),
42→    NOTE_MODIFICATION_TIME("noteMod", 3),
43→    CARD_MODIFICATION_TIME("cardMod", 4),
44→    DUE_TIME("cardDue", 5),
45→    INTERVAL("cardIvl", 6),
46→    EASE("cardEase", 7),
47→    REVIEWS("cardReps", 8),
48→    LAPSES("cardLapses", 9);
49→
50→    fun save(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false) {
51→        // in the case of 'no sorting', we still need a sort type.
52→        // The inverse is handled in `fromCol`
53→        if (isNotesMode) {
54→            config.set("noteSortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
55→        } else {
56→            config.set("sortType", this.ankiSortType ?: SORT_FIELD.ankiSortType)
57→        }
58→        preferences.edit {
59→            putBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", this@SortType == NO_SORTING)
60→        }
61→    }
62→
63→    /** Converts the [SortType] to a [SortOrder] */
64→    fun toSortOrder(): SortOrder =
65→        if (this == NO_SORTING) SortOrder.NoOrdering() else SortOrder.UseCollectionOrdering()
66→
67→    companion object {
68→        fun fromCol(config: Config, preferences: SharedPreferences, isNotesMode: Boolean = false): SortType {
69→            val colOrder = if (isNotesMode) {
70→                config.get<String>("noteSortType")
71→            } else {
72→                config.get<String>("sortType")
73→            }
74→            val type = entries.firstOrNull { it.ankiSortType == colOrder } ?: NO_SORTING
75→            if (type == SORT_FIELD && preferences.getBoolean("cardBrowserNoSorting", false)) {
76→                return NO_SORTING
77→            }
78→            return type
79→        }
80→
81→        fun fromCardBrowserLabelIndex(index: Int): SortType {
82→            return entries.firstOrNull { it.cardBrowserLabelIndex == index } ?: NO_SORTING
83→        }
84→    }
85→}
